=== bobdylan2000-07-15.txt

===================
Bob Dylan
7/15/00 Alpine Valley Music Tre.

(opened for Phil Lesh/ Friends)WideSpread Panic also played...




LB-030 Neumann KM-184s-> Denecke PS-2-> INBOX-> M1, FOB/ Hat/ Rick R. taper/ first stealth effort, DAT-> CDR/ .SHN:, Scott McPherrin hippies@frontiernet.net, M1-> Oade Active I/O-> SEK'D Prodif Plus->, Samplitude->CDWAV-> MKWact/ CDRWIN, THIS RECORDING IS NOT TO BE MP-3 ENCODED!, excellent sound, (opened for Phil Lesh/ Friends), WideSpread Panic also played.; (a different version of this recording was bittorrented 5/05 as "Nice sounding audience recording. Unknown source>cdr>EAC>WAV>FLAC" which looked very similar but did not match up in eac but also had same minor flaws)

but a little distant, blips at Cd1trk2 1:47 trk6 1:20 1:26, negative audience noise on trk6, 8

Duncan And Brady (acoustic), Mr. Tambourine Man (acoustic) (Bob on harp), Desolation Row (acoustic), Boots Of Spanish Leather (acoustic), Tangled Up In Blue (acoustic), Searching For A Soldier's Grave (acoustic) (Larry on mandolin) (song by Johnnie Wright, Jim Anglin and Jack Anglin), Country Pie, Just Like Tom Thumb's Blues, It Takes A Lot To Laugh, It Takes A Train To Cry, Stuck Inside Of Mobile With The Memphis Blues Again, The Wicked Messenger (Bob on harp), Leopard-Skin Pill-Box Hat, (encore), Things Have Changed, Like A Rolling Stone, Don't Think Twice, It's All Right (acoustic) (Bob on harp), Highway 61 Revisited, Blowin' In The Wind (acoustic)
